text.title: nextjs-app-router Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for nextjs-app-router. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Default every component to a Server Component. Add 'use client' only when the component needs state, effects, browser APIs, or event handlers.
rule.rationale: Server Components ship zero client JS by default; premature 'use client' inflates the bundle and disables server-side data access.

rule.statement: Keep 'use client' at the leaves of the component tree — never at layout level unless strictly necessary.
rule.rationale: A 'use client' directive is viral: it converts the entire subtree to client code, negating RSC benefits for unrelated children.

rule.statement: Pass only serializable props across the server/client boundary: primitives, plain objects, arrays. Never pass functions, Date objects, class instances, or Map/Set.
rule.rationale: Non-serializable props cause silent runtime errors when Next.js tries to serialize the server-rendered component tree.

rule.statement: Co-locate data fetching with the component that consumes it (async Server Component + await), not in a parent layout that passes data down.
rule.rationale: Prop-drilling through layouts couples layout structure to data shape; each component fetching its own data removes the coupling and enables parallel requests.

rule.statement: Wrap each slow data fetch in a Suspense boundary with a meaningful skeleton, not a generic spinner.
rule.rationale: Streaming allows fast sections to render while slow ones load; a global spinner blocks everything and wastes RSC's streaming capability.

rule.statement: Use Server Actions for mutations; validate inputs with Zod before any DB call; call revalidatePath/revalidateTag to refresh exactly the affected data.
rule.rationale: Server Actions remove the need for a separate API route for mutations and integrate with progressive enhancement. Zod validation prevents trusting raw formData.

rule.statement: Choose a rendering strategy per route and document it: full SSG (generateStaticParams), ISR (revalidate: N), or dynamic (force-dynamic). Default is static; dynamic rendering opts in by touching cookies()/headers()/searchParams.
rule.rationale: Accidental dynamic rendering is a silent performance regression; auditing next build output for unexpected λ dynamic routes surfaces it.

rule.statement: Every route segment should include loading.tsx (Suspense fallback) and error.tsx (error boundary). Do not delete them — they are required for graceful UX.
rule.rationale: Missing loading.tsx causes the entire segment to block until all data is resolved; missing error.tsx surfaces unhandled errors to users.
